title: Academic Research & Citation: Sentence Frames for Grades 6-8
description: This pack provides academic sentence frames for newcomer ELL students in grades 6-8 to discuss research processes, source evaluation, and proper citation techniques in English and Spanish.

sentence_frames.frame: I need to locate reliable sources for my research on _______________.
sentence_frames.translation: Necesito localizar fuentes confiables para mi investigación sobre _______________.
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A pencil filling in a blank line

sentence_frames.frame: To avoid plagiarism, I must always cite the original source of information.
sentence_frames.translation: Para evitar el plagio, siempre debo citar la fuente original de información.

sentence_frames.frame: When evaluating a source, I consider its authority, accuracy, and purpose.
sentence_frames.translation: Al evaluar una fuente, considero su autoridad, precisión y propósito.

sentence_frames.frame: I will paraphrase this information in my own words to demonstrate understanding.
sentence_frames.translation: Parafrasearé esta información con mis propias palabras para demostrar comprensión.

sentence_frames.frame: Can you explain the difference between a primary and secondary source?
sentence_frames.translation: ¿Puedes explicar la diferencia entre una fuente primaria y una secundaria?
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A question mark in a speech bubble

sentence_frames.frame: According to the article '_______________', the main argument is that _______________.
sentence_frames.translation: Según el artículo '_______________', el argumento principal es que _______________.
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A pencil filling in a blank line

sentence_frames.frame: I am having difficulty understanding how to properly format a citation for _______________.
sentence_frames.translation: Estoy teniendo dificultades para entender cómo formatear correctamente una cita para _______________.
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A pencil filling in a blank line

sentence_frames.frame: My research question is '_______________', and I hope to find evidence to support _______________.
sentence_frames.translation: Mi pregunta de investigación es '_______________', y espero encontrar evidencia que apoye _______________.
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A pencil filling in a blank line

sentence_frames.frame: It is crucial to credit authors for their intellectual property by using citations.
sentence_frames.translation: Es crucial dar crédito a los autores por su propiedad intelectual mediante el uso de citas.

sentence_frames.frame: The evidence from this source helps to strengthen my argument about _______________.
sentence_frames.translation: La evidencia de esta fuente ayuda a fortalecer mi argumento sobre _______________.
sentence_frames.icon_prompt: A pencil filling in a blank line

dialogue_strips.speaker_a_en: I need to locate reliable sources for my research on climate change.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_en: When evaluating a source, I consider its authority, accuracy, and purpose. That might help you!
dialogue_strips.speaker_a_l2: Necesito localizar fuentes confiables para mi investigación sobre el cambio climático.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_l2: Al evaluar una fuente, considero su autoridad, precisión y propósito. ¡Eso podría ayudarte!

dialogue_strips.speaker_a_en: I am having difficulty understanding how to properly format a citation for a website.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_en: To avoid plagiarism, I must always cite the original source of information. Let's look at the example in our textbook together.
dialogue_strips.speaker_a_l2: Estoy teniendo dificultades para entender cómo formatear correctamente una cita para un sitio web.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_l2: Para evitar el plagio, siempre debo citar la fuente original de información. Veamos el ejemplo en nuestro libro de texto juntos.

dialogue_strips.speaker_a_en: My research question is 'How does deforestation impact biodiversity?', and I hope to find evidence to support my claim.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_en: The evidence from this source helps to strengthen my argument about the Amazon rainforest. Maybe you can find something similar.
dialogue_strips.speaker_a_l2: Mi pregunta de investigación es '¿Cómo afecta la deforestación a la biodiversidad?', y espero encontrar evidencia que apoye mi afirmación.
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_l2: La evidencia de esta fuente ayuda a fortalecer mi argumento sobre la selva amazónica. Quizás puedas encontrar algo similar.
dialogue_strips.context: Students sharing their research questions and findings

dialogue_strips.speaker_a_en: Can you explain the difference between a primary and secondary source?
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_en: I will paraphrase this information in my own words to demonstrate understanding, but broadly, primary sources are original, and secondary sources analyze those originals.
dialogue_strips.speaker_a_l2: ¿Puedes explicar la diferencia entre una fuente primaria y una secundaria?
dialogue_strips.speaker_b_l2: Parafrasearé esta información con mis propias palabras para demostrar comprensión, pero en general, las fuentes primarias son originales y las secundarias analizan esos originales.

speaking_prompts.prompt_en: You found an article online. How do you determine if it's a good source for your project?
speaking_prompts.prompt_l2: Encontraste un artículo en línea. ¿Cómo determinas si es una buena fuente para tu proyecto?
speaking_prompts.expected_response: When evaluating a source, I consider its authority, accuracy, and purpose.
speaking_prompts.visual_cue: Image of a student looking at a laptop screen with various article snippets.

speaking_prompts.prompt_en: You've read an interesting fact in a book. What must you do when you include it in your report?
speaking_prompts.prompt_l2: Leíste un dato interesante en un libro. ¿Qué debes hacer cuando lo incluyas en tu informe?
speaking_prompts.expected_response: To avoid plagiarism, I must always cite the original source of information.
speaking_prompts.visual_cue: Image of a book open next to a notebook with a pen.

speaking_prompts.prompt_en: Your teacher asks you to put information into your own words. Why is this important?
speaking_prompts.prompt_l2: Tu maestro te pide que pongas la información en tus propias palabras. ¿Por qué es esto importante?
speaking_prompts.expected_response: I will paraphrase this information in my own words to demonstrate understanding.
speaking_prompts.visual_cue: Image of a thought bubble with original text and another thought bubble with rephrased text.

speaking_prompts.prompt_en: You're starting a new research project. What is the first thing you need to identify?
speaking_prompts.prompt_l2: Estás comenzando un nuevo proyecto de investigación. ¿Qué es lo primero que necesitas identificar?
speaking_prompts.expected_response: My research question is '_______________', and I hope to find evidence to support _______________.
speaking_prompts.visual_cue: Image of a magnifying glass over a question mark.

worksheets.instructions_en: Use the sentence frames to write complete sentences based on the prompts below. Remember to fill in the blanks with your own ideas.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Usa los marcos de oraciones para escribir oraciones completas basadas en las indicaciones a continuación. Recuerda llenar los espacios en blanco con tus propias ideas.

items.content: Write a sentence about what you need for a research project using: 'I need to locate reliable sources for my research on _______________.'
items.correct_answer: Varies by student. Example: I need to locate reliable sources for my research on the history of video games.

items.content: Explain why citing sources is important using: 'To avoid plagiarism, I must always cite the original source of information.'
items.correct_answer: Varies by student. Example: To avoid plagiarism, I must always cite the original source of information, because it is important to give credit to the author.

items.content: Describe how you would evaluate an online article using: 'When evaluating a source, I consider its authority, accuracy, and purpose.'
items.correct_answer: Varies by student. Example: When evaluating a source, I consider its authority, accuracy, and purpose to make sure it's trustworthy.

items.content: Formulate a research question using: 'My research question is '_______________', and I hope to find evidence to support _______________.'
items.correct_answer: Varies by student. Example: My research question is 'Do video games improve cognitive skills?', and I hope to find evidence to support that they do.

worksheets.instructions_en: Complete the sentences by filling in the blanks with the correct word or phrase from the word bank. (Word Bank: plagiarism, reliable, paraphrase, primary, strengthen)
worksheets.instructions_l2: Completa las oraciones llenando los espacios en blanco con la palabra o frase correcta del banco de palabras. (Banco de palabras: plagio, confiables, parafrasear, primaria, fortalecer)

items.content: I need to locate _______________ sources for my research on endangered species.
To avoid _______________, I must always cite the original source of information.
I will _______________ this information in my own words to demonstrate understanding.
Can you explain the difference between a _______________ and secondary source?
The evidence from this source helps to _______________ my argument about renewable energy.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to use academic language frames to discuss research processes, source evaluation, and citation practices in English.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce frames one by one, modeling pronunciation and usage. Practice in pairs or small groups through structured dialogues. Use speaking prompts for whole-class discussion or individual assessment. Worksheets can be used for independent practice or homework. Encourage students to keep a 'Research & Citation' vocabulary journal.
materials_needed: Copies of sentence frames, dialogue strips, speaking prompts, and worksheets
Index cards or small whiteboards for quick responses
Access to computers/internet for example research (optional)
differentiation_tips: **For beginning ELLs:** Provide sentence starters or word banks for blanks. Allow students to use L1 for initial brainstorming. Pair with a more proficient English speaker.
**For intermediate ELLs:** Encourage elaboration on responses. Ask 'why' and 'how' questions. Challenge them to use multiple frames in one response.
**For advanced ELLs:** Have them create their own dialogue strips or speaking prompts. Encourage debate or critical analysis using the frames.
